article.title: Project Aura: Your Next-Gen Open-Source Air Quality Monitor (No Soldering!)
article.body_markdown:
---
title: Project Aura: Your Next-Gen Open-Source Air Quality Monitor (No Soldering!)
published: True
tags: esp32, airquality, iot, homeassistant
---

## Quick Summary: 📝
Project Aura AQ is an open-source ESP32-S3 based air quality station featuring a touch-friendly LVGL UI, local web setup, and MQTT integration for Home Assistant. It provides comprehensive air quality telemetry and is designed for easy assembly by makers.

## Key Takeaways: 💡

* ✅ Project Aura offers comprehensive, professional-grade air quality monitoring with extensive telemetry.

* ✅ Assembly is simplified with no soldering required, utilizing Grove/QT connectors for easy setup.

* ✅ Features a smooth, customizable LVGL touch UI and a powerful web dashboard for live data, charts, and OTA updates.

* ✅ Seamlessly integrates with Home Assistant via automatic MQTT discovery, ideal for smart home automation.

* ✅ Built with reliability in mind, including a 'Safe Boot' feature for automatic configuration rollback.


## Project Statistics: 📊
* ⭐ **Stars:** 366
* 🍴 **Forks:** 30
* ❗ **Open Issues:** 1


## Tech Stack: 💻
- ✅ C


Project Aura is an innovative open-source air quality station designed specifically for makers and developers who desire a polished and reliable device, moving beyond basic sensor boards. It provides a comprehensive solution for monitoring various environmental parameters with a professional-grade user experience.

At its core, Project Aura leverages the powerful ESP32-S3 microcontroller. One of its standout features for makers is the assembly process: it's designed for ease, requiring absolutely no soldering thanks to its reliance on Grove/QT connectors. This significantly lowers the barrier to entry, allowing more developers to jump straight into building and customizing their own advanced air quality monitor.

The device boasts a smooth, touch-friendly user interface built with LVGL. This UI isn't just functional; it's highly customizable, offering features like night mode, custom themes, and clear status indicators, ensuring a pleasant and informative interaction. It transforms raw sensor data into easily digestible visual information directly on the device's screen.

Beyond the on-device display, Project Aura includes an integrated web dashboard, accessible locally. This powerful dashboard provides a live view of all sensor data, historical charts, event logs, and allows for seamless device configuration. Crucially, it also supports Over-The-Air (OTA) firmware updates, making maintenance and feature upgrades incredibly convenient. The telemetry it gathers is extensive, covering PM0.5, PM1, PM2.5, PM4, PM10, CO, CO2, VOC, NOx, temperature, humidity, absolute humidity, pressure, and HCHO.

For smart home enthusiasts, Project Aura is a game-changer. It's Home Assistant ready, featuring automatic MQTT discovery, which means integrating it into your existing smart home setup is a breeze. Additionally, it offers optional DAC control (GP8403, 0-10V), allowing for manual levels, timers, or even automatic demand mode based on air quality thresholds, perfect for controlling ventilation systems.

Developers will appreciate the project's robust 'Safe Boot' feature, which ensures automatic rollback to the last-known-good configuration after any unexpected crashes, enhancing reliability. This project provides a ready-to-use, highly customizable, and robust platform for environmental monitoring. It saves countless hours of development time by providing a stable foundation with advanced features already implemented, making it an excellent starting point for personal projects, smart home integrations, or even as a learning tool for embedded development with ESP32 and LVGL.

## Learn More: 🔗
[View the Project on GitHub](https://github.com/21cncstudio/project_aura)
